trade.summary: Substantial bilateral trade under AGOA (African Growth and Opportunity Act), which grants South Africa preferential access to US markets. South Africa exports vehicles, minerals, precious metals, and fruits to the US; the US exports machinery, aircraft, chemicals, and electronics to South Africa. AGOA renewal is a persistent bilateral trade issue.
disputes: South Africa's land reform policies raise US investor concerns
AGOA renewal uncertainty and South Africa's trade diversification
US concerns over South Africa's BRI engagement and Russia arms trade

diplomatic.summary: US-South Africa relations have been significantly strained in recent years. South Africa's refusal to condemn Russia's invasion of Ukraine, its participation in joint naval exercises with Russia and China, its ICJ genocide case against Israel, and its BRICS deepening have all caused diplomatic friction with the US. The US has periodically threatened to review South Africa's AGOA status. South Africa maintains a principled non-aligned posture that increasingly diverges from US preferences.
recent_incidents: South Africa abstained on UN votes condemning Russia's Ukraine invasion
South Africa hosted Russia-China joint naval exercises (2022-2023)
South Africa filed ICJ genocide case against Israel (2024)
US Ambassador warned South Africa over alleged Russia arms transfers
South Africa joined BRICS and supports BRICS currency discussions

military.summary: No formal alliance. US and South Africa have historically cooperated on African peacekeeping through IMET and other programmes. However, South Africa's Russia-China military exercises and alleged arms transfers to Russia have severely damaged defence cooperation. The US has suspended some military assistance programmes. Engagement through ACOTA (Africa Contingency Operations Training) continues at a reduced level.
incidents: US Ambassador accused South Africa of secretly supplying arms to Russia (2023)
South Africa conducted joint naval exercises with Russia and China (2023)

financial.summary: The US is among South Africa's largest foreign investors, with significant exposure in financial services, mining, technology, and manufacturing. South Africa holds US Treasury bonds as FX reserves. US institutional investors hold South African equities and bonds. Investment has been dampened by South Africa's political risk and electricity crisis.

energy.summary: US energy firms have been involved in South Africa's energy sector, including gas exploration off the Southern Cape. The US has supported South Africa's Just Energy Transition Partnership (JETP) to assist coal phase-out. Growing US LNG interest in the South African market as South Africa diversifies away from coal.

scientific.summary: Strong scientific collaboration in health sciences (HIV/AIDS, TB, malaria research through PEPFAR and NIH), astronomy (Square Kilometre Array), and environmental sciences. The US-South Africa Fulbright programme remains active. Joint research through the South African-US Research Initiative (SAURI) and academic partnerships.
